discovery.run_summary: Risk-on tape with SMH +2.6% and QQQ +1.67% driven by AVGO/TSM strength and SK Hynix record memory results. Notable divergence: networking/edge-connectivity names (CALX, EXTR) selling off hard against a euphoric semi tape, and BBY weak despite consumer resilience. ServiceNow's Iran-war revenue hit is the key narrative creating collateral pressure on software/subscription names.

notable_index_moves: SMH +2.62% led by AVGO +5.09% and TSM +5.26% on SK Hynix record HBM results
ITA -1.77% weak despite geopolitical tension — possible Iran ceasefire optimism fading but defense still sold
XLRE -0.73% and KRE -0.55% lagging; yields likely firm

discoveries.name: Calix, Inc
discoveries.mechanism: Likely earnings-driven gap down (5-day -13.17% matches today, suggesting the news hit today). A -14% move on 4x volume in a broadband-access equipment vendor against a +2.6% SMH tape is a stark idiosyncratic break — often an overshoot in small/mid-cap networking, but could also be guide-down justified.
discoveries.catalyst: Not explicitly in news feed — almost certainly quarterly results/guidance released around this print given magnitude and volume
research_pointers: Pull CALX Q1 results and guidance — check BEAD program commentary and RPO
Compare to prior guide-down pattern (CALX has history of violent reactions to service provider capex commentary)
Check if the weakness in EXTR same day suggests a broader enterprise/service provider networking capex concern vs. company-specific
discoveries.what_would_falsify: If guidance was cut materially on structural demand issues (not just timing), the -14% is justified and not overdone

discoveries.name: Extreme Networks
discoveries.mechanism: Possible sympathy selling with CALX on networking/enterprise capex concerns, against an otherwise strongly bullish semi/tech tape. 3x volume suggests real flow, not just drift.
discoveries.catalyst: No direct news; likely CALX read-through or own pre-announcement
research_pointers: Check if EXTR has an earnings date imminent
Determine whether CALX's customer base (Tier 2/3 telcos) actually overlaps with EXTR (enterprise campus) — if minimal overlap, the sympathy move may be overdone
Scan for any channel checks on enterprise networking demand
discoveries.what_would_falsify: EXTR own guidance confirming enterprise spending softness, or a sector-wide negative preannouncement

discoveries.name: Best Buy
discoveries.mechanism: No direct catalyst in feed. XLY essentially flat (-0.03%), so this is idiosyncratic. Could be tariff-refund headline read-through (Trump Truth Social rant about $159B tariff refunds implies uncertainty on future tariffs/consumer electronics pricing) or analyst action.
discoveries.catalyst: Unknown — possibly tariff/Supreme Court narrative, analyst downgrade, or channel data
research_pointers: Check for analyst downgrades or price target cuts on BBY today
Assess whether tariff refund ruling is net positive or negative for BBY margin/pricing
discoveries.what_would_falsify: A specific downgrade or guide cut would move this to LIKELY RATIONAL

discoveries.name: Repligen
discoveries.mechanism: Bioprocessing name down 6% on only 1.2x volume against XBI +0.67%. Low volume multiple argues against a major catalyst — could be peer/customer commentary (large pharma capex) or positioning unwind.
research_pointers: Check if a major bioprocessing customer (e.g., Sartorius, Danaher, Thermo) provided cautious commentary
Scan biotech funding/IPO activity which drives Repligen demand
discoveries.what_would_falsify: Specific guide-down or customer loss news

discoveries.name: Manhattan Associates
discoveries.mechanism: Up 5.9% on 2x volume, 5-day +10.9% — suggests earnings beat or raised guide. Notable because ServiceNow's Iran-war subscription hit is pressuring SaaS names, yet MANH is bucking hard. If MANH reported strong and guided up, it's a meaningful positive signal for supply-chain software.
research_pointers: Pull MANH Q1 print and RPO/cloud growth rate
Compare MANH commentary to NOW's Iran-related softness — does MANH see same headwind?
Check read-across to peers like SPS Commerce, Descartes
discoveries.what_would_falsify: If the move is purely short-covering on a small beat, durability is low

catalyst_chains.primary_event: SK Hynix record Q1 on HBM/memory pricing surge
ServiceNow -14% on Iran war subscription revenue hit

secondary_opportunities.relationship: peer SaaS (but appears to have decoupled — negative read-through did NOT stick)

trump_signals.post_summary: Trump rails against Supreme Court tariff decision forcing $159B in tariff refunds; signals intent to continue aggressive tariff policy
trump_signals.note: Tariff refund ruling creates uncertainty for importers of consumer electronics. Short-term negative tone from Trump suggests administration will seek new tariff pathways, keeping pressure on consumer electronics retailers. May partially explain BBY weakness today.

ai_announcements.headline: Google Workspace AI update, Chrome auto-browse, Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform, new TPUs
ai_announcements.summary: Google's Cloud Next conference brought a wave of AI-driven productivity and agent tools for enterprise, plus new TPUs competing with Nvidia.

affected_stocks.counter_thesis: Manhattan Associates' supply-chain/WMS software is mission-critical and deeply embedded; Google AI agents are a complement, not a replacement. Rally more likely driven by earnings/supply-chain AI narrative than Google news directly.
research_pointers: Check if MANH reported earnings or guidance today
Correlation of move to specific catalyst vs. Google Cloud Next halo

ai_announcements.headline: SpaceX offers $10B collaboration fee and path to $60B acquisition of Cursor
ai_announcements.summary: Cursor halted $2B funding round after SpaceX's buyout overture. Signals massive strategic value placed on AI coding tools.

ai_analysis.no_signals_note: Today's large movers (CALX -14%, EXTR -5.8%, RGEN -6.3%, BBY -4.6%, VECO +5.3%, WEX +4.2%, GME +4.9%) do not show clear linkage to the AI announcements in the feed. CALX and EXTR moves on high volume look earnings/guidance-driven (networking hardware), not AI-news reactive. RGEN healthcare move unrelated to the small 10x Science seed round. BBY weakness is consumer retail, not AI-driven. No high-conviction AI-mispricing signals identified from this dataset.
